description: Mutable shared state is stashed in the process dictionary or a global instead of a process, causing races and lost updates.
body: Failure mode: code uses Process.put/get, abuses module attributes as if they were variables, or relies on a global to share mutable state across processes, breaking the BEAM's share-nothing isolation and producing race conditions and lost updates under concurrency. Detection signals: Process.put/get holding business state; module attributes mutated at runtime expectations; concurrent updates clobbering each other. Do not fake shared mutable state outside a process. Safer fallback: own the state in a GenServer/Agent mutated via messages, or use an ETS table with clear ownership for high-read shared data, and test that concurrent updates serialize without loss.
